Name: Bellsprout
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its bud looks like a human face. Because of the bud, it is rumored to be a type of legendary mandrake plant.

Name: Weepinbell
Effects: Attach up to 2 [G] Energy cards from your hand to this Pokémon.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It spits out Poison Powder to immobilize the enemy and then finishes it with a spray of Acid.

Name: Victreebel
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: Once ingested into this Pokémon’s body, even the hardest object will melt into nothing.

Name: Scyther
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Scyther and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: While young, they live together deep in the mountains, training themselves in how to fight with their scythes and move at high speeds.

Name: Spinarak
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Some fishermen weave its sturdy thread into nets to catch fish Pokémon.

Name: Ariados
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each Special Condition affecting your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
You may switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It spins thread from both its rear and its mouth. Then it wraps its prey up in thread and sips their bodily fluids at its leisure.

Name: Treecko
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Small hooks on the bottom of its feet catch on walls and ceilings. That is how it can hang from above.

Name: Treecko
Flavor-Text: Small hooks on the bottom of its feet catch on walls and ceilings. That is how it can hang from above.

Name: Grovyle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It lives in dense jungles. While closing in on its prey, it leaps from branch to branch.

Name: Sceptile
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to your Pokémon that have any [G] Energy attached to them by attacks from your opponent’s Ultra Beasts.
Effects: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It agilely leaps about the jungle and uses the sharp leaves on its arms to strike its prey.

Name: Seedot
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks if that damage is 40 or less.
Flavor-Text: When it dangles from a tree branch, it looks just like an acorn. It enjoys scaring other Pokémon.

Name: Seedot
Flavor-Text: When it dangles from a tree branch, it looks just like an acorn. It enjoys scaring other Pokémon.

Name: Nuzleaf
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand. Choose a Supporter card you find there. Your opponent shuffles that card into their deck.
Flavor-Text: It lives deep in forests. With the leaf on its head, it makes a flute whose song makes listeners uneasy.

Name: Shiftry-GX
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 90 more damage.
Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. Your opponent shuffles that Pokémon and all cards attached to it into their deck. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Surskit
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: When this Pokémon senses danger, a sweet fluid oozes from the tip of its head. The taste of it disgusts bird Pokémon.

Name: Masquerain
Effects: Discard all Special Energy from each of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Its wings and antennae don’t cope well with moisture. After a rain, it faces sunward to dry off.

Name: Volbeat
Effects: If your Illumise used Pheromone Signals during your last turn, this attack does 100 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It lives around clean ponds. At night, its rear lights up. It converses with others by flashing its light.

Name: Illumise
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: With its sweet aroma, it guides Volbeat to draw signs with light in the night sky.

Name: Cacnea
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It prefers harsh environments such as deserts. It can survive for 30 days on water stored in its body.

Name: Cacturne
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This damage isn’t affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Packs of them follow travelers through the desert until the travelers can no longer move.

Name: Tropius
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: The bunch of fruit around its neck ripens twice a year and is delicious. It’s a highly favored tropical snack.

Name: Dhelmise
Effects: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
This Pokémon can’t attack during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: The soul of seaweed adrift in the waves became reborn as this Pokémon. It maintains itself with new infusions of seabed detritus and seaweed.

Name: Slugma
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Its body is made of magma. If it doesn’t keep moving, its body will cool and harden.

Name: Magcargo
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a card, shuffle your deck, then put that card on top of it.
Flavor-Text: Its body is as hot as lava and is always billowing. Flames will occasionally burst from its shell.

Name: Torchic
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A fire burns inside, so it feels very warm to hug. It launches fireballs of 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Torchic
Flavor-Text: A fire burns inside, so it feels very warm to hug. It launches fireballs of 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Combusken
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: During a battle, the hot flame in its body increases. Its kicks have outstanding destructive power.

Name: Blaziken-GX
Effects: Discard 2 [R] Energy from this Pokémon.
Discard 2 Energy from your opponent’s Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Torkoal
Effects: Discard the top 4 cards of your deck. If any of those cards are [R] Energy cards, attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: If the fire burning within its shell goes out, it will die. Those who wish to raise one in their home must always keep something flammable at hand.

Name: Oricorio
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. The new Active Pokémon is now Burned and Confused.
Flavor-Text: This Oricorio has sipped red nectar. Its passionate dance moves cause its enemies to combust in both body and mind.

Name: Articuno-GX
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may switch it with your Active Pokémon. If you do, move any number of [W] Energy from your other Pokémon to this Pokémon.
Effects: Discard all Energy from both Active Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mudkip
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 [W] Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: To alert it, the fin on its head senses the flow of water. It has the strength to heft boulders.

Name: Mudkip
Flavor-Text: To alert it, the fin on its head senses the flow of water. It has the strength to heft boulders.

Name: Marshtomp
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Living on muddy ground that provides poor footing has made its legs sturdy.

Name: Swampert
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a card from your hand. If you do, draw 3 cards.
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It can swim while towing a large ship. It bashes down foes with a swing of its thick arms.

Name: Lotad
Effects: Choose a random card from your opponent’s hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.
Flavor-Text: It searches about for clean water. If it does not drink water for too long, the leaf on its head wilts.

Name: Lombre
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any [W] Energy attached to it, it has no Retreat Cost.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It has a mischievous spirit. If it spots an angler, it will tug on the fishing line to interfere.

Name: Ludicolo
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may draw a card.
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each other Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s).
Flavor-Text: The rhythm of bright, festive music activates Ludicolo’s cells, making it more powerful.

Name: Wailmer
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, heal 50 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It shows off by spraying jets of seawater from the nostrils above its eyes. It eats a solid ton of Wishiwashi every day.

Name: Wailord
Effects: This attack does 40 less damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its immense size is the reason for its popularity. Wailord watching is a favorite sightseeing activity in various parts of the world.

Name: Clamperl
Ability-Effect: If you go second, this Pokémon can evolve during your first turn.
Flavor-Text: It is protected by a sturdy shell. Once in a lifetime, it makes a magnificent pearl.

Name: Huntail
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It lives deep in the sea. With a tail shaped like a small fish, it attracts unsuspecting prey.

Name: Gorebyss
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Evolution Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its swimming form is exquisitely elegant. With its thin mouth, it feeds on seaweed that grows between rocks.

Name: Luvdisc
Effects: Search your deck for a number of Basic Pokémon up to the number of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon and put those Pokémon onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Loving couples have a soft spot for this Pokémon, so honeymoon hotels often release this Pokémon into their pools.

Name: Regice
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, your opponent can’t play any Stadium cards from their hand.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Its body is made of ice from the ice age. It controls frigid air of -328 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Kyogre
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
This Pokémon can’t use Grand Wave during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: It is said to have widened the seas by causing downpours. It had been asleep in a marine trench.

Name: Voltorb
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any Energy attached to it, it has no Retreat Cost.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Usually found in power plants. Easily mistaken for a Poké Ball, it has zapped many people.

Name: Electrode-GX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach 5 Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon, except Pokémon-GX or Pokémon-EX, in any way you like. If you do, this Pokémon is Knocked Out.
Effects: Discard any amount of Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 50 more damage for each card you discarded in this way. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Chinchou
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It lives in the depths beyond the reach of sunlight. It flashes lights on its antennae to communicate with others of its kind.

Name: Lanturn
Ability-Effect: When 1 of your Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack, you may move a basic Energy card from that Pokémon to this Pokémon.
Effects: You may discard all [L] Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon flashes a bright light that blinds its prey. This creates an opening for it to deliver an electrical attack.

Name: Electrike
Flavor-Text: It stores static electricity in its fur for discharging. It gives off sparks if a storm approaches.

Name: Manectric
Ability-Effect: If you go second, and if this Pokémon is in your hand when you are setting up to play, you may put it face down as your Active Pokémon or on your Bench.
Effects: You may attach up to 2 basic Energy cards from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It discharges electricity from its mane. It creates a thundercloud overhead to drop lightning bolts.

Name: Plusle
Effects: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw a card for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s).
Flavor-Text: It absorbs electricity from telephone poles. It shorts out its body to create crackling noises.

Name: Minun
Effects: You may discard any number of cards from your hand. Then, draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand.
Flavor-Text: It cheers on friends. If its friends are losing, its body lets off more and more sparks.

Name: Oricorio
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to each Pokémon-GX and Pokémon-EX (both yours and your opponent’s). (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It creates an electric charge by rubbing its feathers together. It dances over to its enemies and delivers shocking electrical punches.

Name: Mr. Mime-GX
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by your opponent’s attacks if that damage is exactly 20, 40, 60, 80, 100, 120, 140, 160, 180, 200, 220, 240, or 260.
Effects: For each card in your opponent’s hand, put 1 damage counter on their Active Pokémon.
Heal all damage from this Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Gulpin
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: There is nothing its stomach can’t digest. While it is digesting, vile, overpowering gases are expelled.

Name: Swalot
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s attacks. That Pokémon can’t use that attack during your opponent’s next turn.
If, before doing damage, your opponent’s Active Pokémon has less remaining HP than this Pokémon, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It gulps anything that fits in its mouth. Its special enzymes can dissolve anything.

Name: Spoink
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It bounces around on its tail to keep its heart pumping. It carries a pearl from Clamperl on its head.

Name: Grumpig
Effects: If 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon in play has the same name as 1 of your Pokémon in play, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It uses black pearls to amplify its psychic power. It does an odd dance to gain control over foes.

Name: Lunatone
Ability-Effect: If you have Solrock in play, [R] Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s) have no Abilities, except Pokémon-GX and Pokémon-EX.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It was discovered at the site of a meteor strike 40 years ago. Its stare can lull its foes to sleep.

Name: Solrock
Ability-Effect: The maximum HP of each of your Lunatone in play is 130.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: It absorbs solar energy during the day. Always expressionless, it can sense what its foe is thinking.

Name: Shuppet
Flavor-Text: It loves vengeful emotions and hangs in rows under the eaves of houses where vengeful people live.

Name: Shuppet
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It loves vengeful emotions and hangs in rows under the eaves of houses where vengeful people live.

Name: Banette
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may put a Basic Pokémon from your opponent’s discard pile onto their Bench.
Effects: For each of your opponent’s Pokémon in play, put 1 damage counter on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: Strong feelings of hatred turned a puppet into a Pokémon. If it opens its mouth, its cursed energy escapes.

Name: Banette-GX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, you may move 1 damage counter from 1 Pokémon to another Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each Supporter card in your discard pile. You can’t add more than 100 damage in this way.
Put 3 cards from your discard pile into your hand. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Deoxys
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: DNA from a space virus mutated and became a Pokémon. It appears where auroras are seen.

Name: Deoxys
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 40 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: DNA from a space virus mutated and became a Pokémon. It appears where auroras are seen.

Name: Deoxys
Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
Flavor-Text: DNA from a space virus mutated and became a Pokémon. It appears where auroras are seen.

Name: Lunala
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any [P] Energy attached to it, it takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is said to be a female evolution of Cosmog. When its third eye activates, away it flies to another world.

Name: Onix
Effects: During your next turn, the Defending Pokémon takes 20 more damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It usually lives underground. It searches for food while boring its way through the ground at 50 miles per hour.

Name: Phanpy
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, if this Pokémon would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent’s next turn, it is not Knocked Out, and its remaining HP becomes 10.
Flavor-Text: It is strong despite its compact size. It can easily pick up and carry an adult human on its back.

Name: Donphan
Effects: This attack does 10 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The longer and bigger its tusks, the higher its rank in its herd. The tusks take long to grow.

Name: Larvitar
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Born deep underground, it comes aboveground and becomes a pupa once it has finished eating the surrounding soil.

Name: Pupitar
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to each non-[F] Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s). (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Its shell is as hard as sheet rock, and it is also very strong. Its thrashing can topple a mountain.

Name: Meditite
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, if this Pokémon would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent’s next turn, it is not Knocked Out, and its remaining HP becomes 10.
Flavor-Text: It eats just one berry a day. By enduring hunger, its spirit is tempered and made sharper.

Name: Medicham
Effects: If this Pokémon’s remaining HP is 30 or less, this attack does 160 more damage.
This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Through yoga training, it gained the psychic power to predict its foe’s next move.

Name: Baltoy
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It moves by spinning on its foot. It is a rare Pokémon that was discovered in ancient ruins.

Name: Claydol
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
This attack does 40 damage for each Steven’s Resolve card in your discard pile.
Flavor-Text: It is said that it originates from clay dolls made by an ancient civilization.

Name: Regirock
Effects: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 20 more damage.
Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: The same rocks that form its body have been found in ground layers around the world.

Name: Groudon
Effects: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 50 more damage. Then, discard that Stadium card.
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This legendary Pokémon is said to represent the land. It went to sleep after dueling Kyogre.

Name: Palossand-GX
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

Look at the top 13 cards of your opponent’s deck and discard any number of Pokémon you find there. This attack does 60 damage for each card you discarded in this way. Your opponent shuffles the other cards back into their deck. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Minior
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is in your hand and your Bench isn’t full, you may move your Active Pokémon to your Bench and play this Pokémon as your new Active Pokémon.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Strong impacts can knock it out of its shell. This Pokémon was born from mutated nanoparticles.

Name: Alolan Rattata
Effects: Search your deck for Alolan Raticate or Alolan Raticate-GX, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: When the sun goes down, it becomes active. It runs around town on a chase for good food for the boss of its nest—Raticate.

Name: Alolan Raticate-GX
Effects: Discard up to 2 cards from your hand. This attack does 40 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Search your deck for up to 6 Item cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Sneasel
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flip a coin for each Pokémon you have in play. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It uses its claws to poke holes in eggs so it can slurp out the insides. Breeders consider it a scourge and will drive it away or eradicate it.

Name: Tyranitar
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.
For each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s), flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 damage to that Pokémon. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
Flavor-Text: If it rampages, it knocks down mountains and buries rivers. Maps must be redrawn afterward.

Name: Sableye
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at the top card of your deck. You may discard that card.
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s attacks. That Pokémon can’t use that attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is feared. When its gemstone eyes begin to glow with a sinister shine, it’s believed that Sableye will steal people’s spirits away.

Name: Steelix
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It chews its way through boulders with its sturdy jaws. Its eyes can see in the darkness underground.

Name: Scizor-GX
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon’s remaining HP is 100 or less, its attacks do 80 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 100 more damage. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mawile
Effects: Search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. If that card is a Pokémon Tool card, instead of putting it into your hand, you may attach it to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon-GX or a Pokémon-EX, this attack does 30 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Attached to its head is a huge set of jaws formed by horns. It can chew through iron beams.

Name: Beldum
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, its Retreat Cost is [C] less for each Beldum on your Bench.
Flavor-Text: With magnetic traction, it pulls its opponents in close. When they’re in range, it slashes them with its rear claws.

Name: Beldum
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: With magnetic traction, it pulls its opponents in close. When they’re in range, it slashes them with its rear claws.

Name: Metang
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It adores magnetic minerals, so it pursues Nosepass at speeds exceeding 60 mph.

Name: Metagross
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, your turn does not end when you play Steven’s Resolve.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Meteor Mash attack does 60 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: A linkage of two Metang, this Pokémon can perform any calculation in a flash by utilizing parallel processing in its four brains.

Name: Registeel
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon has an Ability, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its body is said to be harder than any kind of metal. A study has revealed that its body is hollow.

Name: Jirachi Prism Star
Ability-Effect: If you took this Pokémon as a face-down Prize card during your turn and your Bench isn’t full, before you put it into your hand, you may put it onto your Bench and take 1 more Prize card.
Effects: This Pokémon is now Asleep. At the end of your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon will be Knocked Out.
Prism-Star-Rule: You can’t have more than 1 [*] card with the same name in your deck. If a [*] card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.
Flavor-Text: It is said to have the ability to grant any wish for just one week every thousand years.

Name: Heatran
Effects: Look at the top 4 cards of your deck and attach any number of [M] Energy cards you find there to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Boiling blood, like magma, circulates through its body. It makes its dwelling place in volcanic caves.

Name: Solgaleo
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any [M] Energy attached to it, it has no Weakness.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: It is said to live in another world. The intense light it radiates from the surface of its body can make the darkest of nights light up like midday.

Name: Celesteela
Effects: If the total of both players’ remaining Prize cards is exactly 6, this attack can be used for [M].
Flavor-Text: One kind of Ultra Beast. Witnesses have seen it burn down a forest by expelling gas from its two arms.

Name: Kartana
Effects: If your opponent has exactly 6 Prize cards remaining, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: One of the Ultra Beast life-forms, it was observed cutting down a gigantic steel tower with one stroke of its blade.

Name: Stakataka-GX
Ability-Effect: Your Ultra Beasts take 10 less damage from your opponent’s attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each Prize card you have taken. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Bagon
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 10 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: With its steel-hard stone head, it headbutts indiscriminately. This is because of the stress it feels at being unable to fly.

Name: Bagon
Effects: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: With its steel-hard stone head, it headbutts indiscriminately. This is because of the stress it feels at being unable to fly.

Name: Shelgon
Effects: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: They lurk deep within caves—motionless, neither eating nor drinking. Why they don’t die is not known.

Name: Salamence
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, once during your turn (before your attack), you may switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It flies around on its wings, which have grown in at last. In its happiness, it gushes hot flames, burning up the fields it passes over.

Name: Latias Prism Star
Effects: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to each of your Basic Benched [N] Pokémon.
Prism-Star-Rule: You can’t have more than 1 [*] card with the same name in your deck. If a [*] card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.
Flavor-Text: It can telepathically communicate with people. It changes its appearance using its down that refracts light.

Name: Latios Prism Star
Effects: This attack does 50 damage for each of your Evolution [N] Pokémon in play.
Prism-Star-Rule: You can’t have more than 1 [*] card with the same name in your deck. If a [*] card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.
Flavor-Text: It understands human speech and is highly intelligent. It is a tender Pokémon that dislikes fighting.

Name: Rayquaza-GX
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may discard the top 3 cards of your deck. If you do, attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 30 damage times the amount of basic [G] and basic [L] Energy attached to your Pokémon.
Discard your hand and draw 10 cards. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Dunsparce
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck. If you put any Pokémon onto your Bench in this way, you may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It digs into the ground with its tail and makes a mazelike nest. It can fly just a little.

Name: Wingull
Flavor-Text: Fishermen keep an eye out for Wingull in the sky, because wherever they’re circling, the ocean is sure to be teeming with fish Pokémon.

Name: Pelipper
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Gathering food is the work of young males. They store food in their capacious beaks and carry it back to others waiting in the nest.

Name: Slakoth
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Heal all damage from this Pokémon. It can’t attack during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: If it eats just three leaves in a day, it is satisfied. Other than that, it sleeps for 20 hours a day.

Name: Vigoroth
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its stress level rises if it cannot keep moving constantly. Too much stress makes it feel sick.

Name: Slaking
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, your opponent’s Pokémon in play have no Abilities, except for Lazy.
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon. It can’t attack during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: It is the world’s most slothful Pokémon. However, it can exert horrifying power by releasing pent-up energy all at once.

Name: Whismur
Effects: You can use this attack only if you go second, and only on your first turn. Your opponent can’t play any Trainer cards from their hand during their next turn.
Flavor-Text: If it senses danger, it scares the foe by crying out with the volume of a jet-plane engine.

Name: Whismur
Effects: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: If it senses danger, it scares the foe by crying out with the volume of a jet-plane engine.

Name: Loudred
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: The shock waves from its cries can tip over trucks. It stamps its feet to power up.

Name: Exploud
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Its roar in battle shakes the ground like a tremor—or like an earthquake has struck.

Name: Skitty
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It shows its cute side by chasing its own tail until it gets dizzy.

Name: Delcatty
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may put 2 Supporter cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: It is highly popular among female Trainers for its sublime fur. It does not keep a nest.

Name: Kecleon
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon has Unit Energy [L][P][M] attached to it, it is a [L], [P], and [M] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It changes its shading to match its surroundings so it can sneak up on prey. Only its belly patterns stay fixed.

Name: Acro Bike
Description: Look at the top 2 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. Discard the other card.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Apricorn Maker
Description: Search your deck for up to 2 Item cards that have the word “Ball” in their name, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Beast Ball
Description: Look at your face-down Prize cards. You may reveal an Ultra Beast card you find there, put it into your hand, and put this Beast Ball in its place. (If you don’t reveal an Ultra Beast card, put this card in the discard pile.) Then, shuffle your face-down Prize cards.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Bill’s Maintenance
Description: Shuffle a card from your hand into your deck. If you do, draw 3 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Copycat
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw a card for each card in your opponent’s hand.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Energy Recycle System
Description: Choose 1:
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Energy Switch
Description: Move a basic Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Fisherman
Description: Put 4 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Friend Ball
Description: Search your deck for a Pokémon with the same type as 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon in play, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Hau
Description: Draw 3 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Hiker
Description: Look at the top 5 cards of either player’s deck and choose 1 of them. That player shuffles the other cards back into their deck. Then, put the card you chose on top of that deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Hustle Belt
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to has 30 HP or less remaining and has any damage counters on it, its attacks do 60 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Last Chance Potion
Description: Heal 120 damage from 1 of your Pokémon that has 30 HP or less remaining.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Life Herb
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, heal 60 damage and remove all Special Conditions from 1 of your Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Lisia
Description: Search your deck for up to 2 [*] (Prism Star) cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Lure Ball
Description: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, put an Evolution Pokémon from your discard pile into your hand.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: The Masked Royal
Description: Attach a basic Energy card from your hand to one of your Stage 2 [G], [R], or [W] Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: PokéNav
Description: Look at the top 3 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Pokémon or Energy card you find there and put it into your hand. Put the other cards back in any order.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Rainbow Brush
Description: Choose an Energy card attached to 1 of your Pokémon. Search your deck for a basic Energy card and switch it with that card. Shuffle the first Energy card into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Rare Candy
Description: Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 2 card in your hand that evolves from that Pokémon, put that card onto the Basic Pokémon to evolve it. You can’t use this card during your first turn or on a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Shrine of Punishment
Description: Between turns, put 1 damage counter on each Pokémon-GX and Pokémon-EX (both yours and your opponent’s).
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Sky Pillar
Description: Prevent all effects of the opponent’s attacks, including damage, done to Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s).
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Steven’s Resolve
Description: Search your deck for up to 3 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. Your turn ends.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Super Scoop Up
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, put 1 of your Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your hand.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Switch
Description: Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Tate & Liza
Description: Choose 1:
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: TV Reporter
Description: Draw 3 cards. Then, discard a card from your hand. If you have no cards in your deck, you can’t play this card.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Underground Expedition
Description: Look at the bottom 4 cards of your deck and put 2 of them into your hand. Put the other cards back on the bottom of your deck in any order.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Rainbow Energy
Description: This card provides [C] Energy. While in play, this card provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time. When you attach this card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon, put 1 damage counter on that Pokémon.

Name: Shiftry-GX
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 90 more damage.
Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. Your opponent shuffles that Pokémon and all cards attached to it into their deck. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Blaziken-GX
Effects: Discard 2 [R] Energy from this Pokémon.
Discard 2 Energy from your opponent’s Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Articuno-GX
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may switch it with your Active Pokémon. If you do, move any number of [W] Energy from your other Pokémon to this Pokémon.
Effects: Discard all Energy from both Active Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Electrode-GX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach 5 Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon, except Pokémon-GX or Pokémon-EX, in any way you like. If you do, this Pokémon is Knocked Out.
Effects: Discard any amount of Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 50 more damage for each card you discarded in this way. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mr. Mime-GX
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by your opponent’s attacks if that damage is exactly 20, 40, 60, 80, 100, 120, 140, 160, 180, 200, 220, 240, or 260.
Effects: For each card in your opponent’s hand, put 1 damage counter on their Active Pokémon.
Heal all damage from this Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Banette-GX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, you may move 1 damage counter from 1 Pokémon to another Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each Supporter card in your discard pile. You can’t add more than 100 damage in this way.
Put 3 cards from your discard pile into your hand. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Scizor-GX
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon’s remaining HP is 100 or less, its attacks do 80 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 100 more damage. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Stakataka-GX
Ability-Effect: Your Ultra Beasts take 10 less damage from your opponent’s attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each Prize card you have taken. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Sceptile
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to your Pokémon that have any [G] Energy attached to them by attacks from your opponent’s Ultra Beasts.
Effects: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It agilely leaps about the jungle and uses the sharp leaves on its arms to strike its prey.

Name: Scyther
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Scyther and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: While young, they live together deep in the mountains, training themselves in how to fight with their scythes and move at high speeds.

Name: Voltorb
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any Energy attached to it, it has no Retreat Cost.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Usually found in power plants. Easily mistaken for a Poké Ball, it has zapped many people.

Name: Shuppet
Flavor-Text: It loves vengeful emotions and hangs in rows under the eaves of houses where vengeful people live.

Name: Celesteela
Effects: If the total of both players’ remaining Prize cards is exactly 6, this attack can be used for [M].
Flavor-Text: One kind of Ultra Beast. Witnesses have seen it burn down a forest by expelling gas from its two arms.

Name: Kartana
Effects: If your opponent has exactly 6 Prize cards remaining, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: One of the Ultra Beast life-forms, it was observed cutting down a gigantic steel tower with one stroke of its blade.
